source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for Custom Role Changes

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for Custom Role Changes
input_row.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for changes to Identity and Access Management (IAM) role creation, deletion and updating activities.
input_row.rationale: Google Cloud IAM provides predefined roles that give granular access to specific Google Cloud Platform resources and prevent unwanted access to other resources. However, to cater to organization-specific needs, Cloud IAM also provides the ability to create custom roles. Project owners and administrators with the Organization Role Administrator role or the IAM Role Administrator role can create custom roles. Monitoring role creation, deletion and updating activities will help in identifying any over- privileged role at early stages. Impact: Enabling of logging may result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage.
input_row.audit: From Console: Ensure that the prescribed log metric is present: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. In the User-defined Metrics section, ensure that at least one metric <Log_Metric_Name> is present with filter text: resource.type="iam_role" AND (protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.CreateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.DeleteRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UpdateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UndeleteRole") Ensure that the prescribed alerting policy is present: 3. Go to Alerting by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/monitoring/alerting. 4. Under the Policies section, ensure that at least one alert policy exists for the log metric above. Clicking on the policy should show that it is configured with a condition. For example, Violates when: Any logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name> stream is above a threshold of zero(0) for greater than zero(0) seconds means that the alert will trigger for any new owner change. Verify that the chosen alerting thresholds make sense for the user's organization. 5. Ensure that the appropriate notifications channels have been set up. From Google Cloud CLI Ensure that the prescribed log metric is present: 1. List the log metrics: gcloud logging metrics list --format json 2. Ensure that the output contains at least one metric with the filter set to: resource.type="iam_role" AND (protoPayload.methodName = "google.iam.admin.v1.CreateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.DeleteRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UpdateRole OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UndeleteRole") 3. Note the value of the property metricDescriptor.type for the identified metric, in the format logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>. Ensure that the prescribed alerting policy is present: 4. List the alerting policies: gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json 5. Ensure that the output contains an least one alert policy where: • conditions.conditionThreshold.filter is set to metric.type=\"logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>\" • AND enabled is set to true.
input_row.remediation: From Console: Create the prescribed log metric: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics and click "CREATE METRIC". 2. Click the down arrow symbol on the Filter Bar at the rightmost corner and select Convert to Advanced Filter. 3. Clear any text and add: resource.type="iam_role" AND (protoPayload.methodName =  "google.iam.admin.v1.CreateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.DeleteRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UpdateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UndeleteRole") 1. Click Submit Filter. Display logs appear based on the filter text entered by the user. 2. In the Metric Editor menu on the right, fill out the name field. Set Units to 1 (default) and Type to Counter. This ensures that the log metric counts the number of log entries matching the advanced logs query. 3. Click Create Metric. Create a prescribed Alert Policy: 1. Identify the new metric that was just created under the section User-defined Metrics at https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. Click the 3-dot icon in the rightmost column for the metric and select Create alert from Metric. A new page displays. 3. Fill out the alert policy configuration and click Save. Choose the alerting threshold and configuration that makes sense for the user's organization. For example, a threshold of zero(0) for the most recent value ensures that a notification is triggered for every owner change in the project: Set `Aggregator` to `Count` Set `Configuration`: - Condition: above - Threshold: 0 - For: most recent value 1. Configure the desired notification channels in the section Notifications. 2. Name the policy and click Save. From Google Cloud CLI Create the prescribed Log Metric: • Use the command: gcloud logging metrics create Create the prescribed Alert Policy: • Use the command: gcloud alpha monitoring policies create References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/logs-based-metrics/ 2. https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/custom-metrics/ 3. https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/alerts/ 4. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/reference/tools/gcloud-logging 5. https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/understanding-custom-roles
input_row.impact: Enabling of logging may result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The control can be verified using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud), which makes it suitable for automation. The necessary log metrics and alerting policies can be checked via GCP CLI commands as outlined in the audit section, providing a clear path for automated assessment.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_metric_and_alert_custom_role_changes

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

To automate this check for the existence of log metric filters and alerting policies for custom role changes, the following steps need to be performed:

1. **Log Metric Verification:**
   - Use the `gcloud logging metrics list --format json` command to list all user-defined log metrics.
   - Check if there is at least one metric with the filter:
     ```
     resource.type="iam_role" AND (protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.CreateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.DeleteRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UpdateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UndeleteRole")
     ```
   - Verify that the `metricDescriptor.type` matches the format: `logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>`.

2. **Alerting Policy Verification:**
   - Use the `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json` command to list all alerting policies.
   - Check for an alerting policy where:
     - `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` is set to `metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>"`.
     - `enabled` is set to `true`.
     - The alert triggers on the metric condition with a threshold of zero for any metric above zero seconds.

3. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - **Pass** if at least one log metric and one corresponding alerting policy exist and meet the specified configuration.
   - **Fail** if no such log metric or alerting policy is identified.

By automating this audit approach, it becomes efficient to continually verify compliance with this control across GCP projects, ensuring that custom role changes are appropriately monitored and alerted upon.
step2_review:
---
### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as "Automated" is correct. Both log metric and alerting policy existence can be programmatically checked using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). There is no need for manual review unless the configuration is highly customized or the automation fails.

- **GCP SDK/CLI/API Suitability:**  
  - **Log Metrics:** `gcloud logging metrics list` (or via Logging API `projects.metrics.list`)
  - **Alerting Policies:** `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` (or Monitoring API `projects.alertPolicies.list`)
  - **CloudFormation:** Not applicable to GCP.
  - **GCP Config:** Not directly relevant; GCP Config is not a service like AWS Config.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 8/10**

- **Strengths:**
  - Correct use of `gcloud logging metrics list --format json` to enumerate log-based metrics.
  - Correct use of `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json` to enumerate alerting policies.
  - The log filter string for custom IAM role changes is accurate and matches the CIS benchmark.
  - The check for metric and alerting policy existence is sound.

- **Weaknesses / Gaps:**
  - **gcloud alpha monitoring policies:**  
    The `gcloud alpha monitoring policies` command is in alpha and may not be enabled in all environments. The preferred stable command is `gcloud monitoring policies list`.
  - **Alerting Policy Matching:**  
    The check for `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` is a bit simplistic. In practice, the alerting policy may reference the log metric by resource type and metric name, not just by metric type string. The filter may also include additional labels or filters.
  - **Threshold Logic:**  
    The description "threshold of zero for any metric above zero seconds" is ambiguous. The typical configuration is to alert if the metric count is `>= 1` over a short period (e.g., 1 minute), not "above zero seconds."
  - **Program Name:**  
    The program name `gcp_logging_metric_and_alert_custom_role_changes` is descriptive, but could be more precise (see below).

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Use Stable CLI:**  
  Replace `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` with `gcloud monitoring policies list` for stability and broader support.

- **Alerting Policy Details:**  
  - When matching alerting policies, ensure the check accounts for:
    - The correct metric type (`logging.googleapis.com/user/<metric_name>`)
    - The threshold value (should be `>= 1` for count-based log metrics)
    - The condition is enabled
    - The notification channel is configured (optional but best practice)
  - Consider parsing the `conditionThreshold.filter` to ensure it matches the intended log metric, not just any log-based metric.

- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  - For clarity and convention, consider:  
    `gcp_logging_metric_alert_custom_iam_role_changes`
  - This makes it clear the metric and alert are for **custom IAM role changes**.

- **Missing Considerations:**
  - **Scope:**  
    Ensure the check is performed at the correct project or organization level.
  - **Multiple Metrics/Policies:**  
    There may be multiple metrics/policies; ensure the logic accounts for this and does not fail if there are multiple valid matches.
  - **API Alternative:**  
    For large environments, consider using the Logging and Monitoring REST APIs directly for more granular queries.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The approach is fundamentally sound and technically feasible with GCP APIs and CLI.
- Minor improvements are needed for robustness and clarity, but the core logic is correct.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **MODIFY**

- **Keep as Automated.**
- **Modify** the automation details as follows:
  - Use `gcloud monitoring policies list` instead of the alpha command.
  - Clarify the alerting policy threshold logic (should be count >= 1, not "above zero seconds").
  - Refine the matching logic for alerting policies to ensure the correct metric is referenced.
  - Update the program name to `gcp_logging_metric_alert_custom_iam_role_changes` for clarity and convention.

---

**Summary Table**

| Section                | Result / Score      | Notes                                                                                   |
|------------------------|--------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Approach Validation    | AGREE              | Automated is correct; can be done via GCP CLI/API.                                      |
| Technical Accuracy     | 8/10               | Minor issues: alpha command, alert threshold logic, program name.                       |
| Improvements           | See above          | Use stable CLI, clarify logic, update naming.                                           |
| Confidence             | HIGH               | Approach is fundamentally sound.                                                        |
| Final Recommendation   | MODIFY             | Update CLI usage, clarify logic, improve naming.                                        |

---

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that the existence of log-based metrics and alerting policies for custom IAM role changes can be programmatically verified using the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) and GCP APIs. The required properties (log metric filters and alerting policy conditions) are fully exposed via these APIs, and there is no need for manual intervention unless automation fails. The only minor correction is to use the stable `gcloud monitoring policies list` command instead of the alpha version.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_logging_log_metric_and_alert_custom_role_changes

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP Resource/Service: Cloud Logging (Log-based Metrics), Cloud Monitoring (Alerting Policies)
- API/CLI Method:
  - List log-based metrics: `gcloud logging metrics list --format=json`
  - List alerting policies: `gcloud monitoring policies list --format=json`
- Properties to Check:
  - Log Metric: Presence of a metric with filter  
    ```
    resource.type="iam_role" AND (protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.CreateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.DeleteRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UpdateRole" OR protoPayload.methodName="google.iam.admin.v1.UndeleteRole")
    ```
  - Alerting Policy: Presence of a policy with a condition that references the above log metric (i.e., `metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>"`)
- Pass Criteria:
  - At least one log-based metric exists with the exact filter for custom IAM role changes.
  - At least one alerting policy exists that triggers on the above metric.
- Fail Criteria:
  - No log-based metric with the required filter exists, or
  - No alerting policy is configured to alert on the required metric.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on automation, with the second opinion providing a minor correction regarding the use of the stable `gcloud monitoring policies list` command. This correction is adopted in the final answer.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
